# GitHub Copilot Instructions for Multi-Agent Development Environment

## Overview
This document provides guidelines for effectively utilizing GitHub Copilot within the Multi-Agent Development Environment project. Each role in the project can leverage the capabilities of AI models to enhance productivity and streamline workflows.

## Roles and Usage

### Full Stack Leader (Claude Opus 4.6)
- **Task Management**: Use Copilot to generate task lists and prioritize development activities.
- **Code Suggestions**: Leverage Copilot for code snippets and best practices in full-stack development.
- **Documentation**: Utilize Copilot to draft and refine documentation related to the development process.

### Researcher (Gemini 3.1 Pro)
- **Information Gathering**: Use Copilot to assist in formulating search queries and summarizing research findings.
- **Data Analysis**: Leverage Copilot to generate code for data analysis and visualization.
- **Literature Review**: Utilize Copilot to draft summaries of relevant literature and extract key insights.

### Assistant (Claude Sonnet 4.6)
- **Task Support**: Use Copilot to automate repetitive tasks and generate boilerplate code.
- **Information Retrieval**: Leverage Copilot to quickly find and summarize information from various sources.
- **Collaboration**: Utilize Copilot to facilitate communication between roles by generating meeting notes and action items.

### Code Architect (GPT-5.2 Codex Max)
- **Architecture Design**: Use Copilot to scaffold system architecture blueprints, ADRs, and technical design documents.
- **Complex Code Generation**: Leverage Copilot to produce optimized, production-grade implementations for critical system components.
- **Security Review**: Utilize Copilot to identify and remediate security vulnerabilities in line with OWASP Top 10 guidelines.

### Code Specialist (GPT-5.3 Codex)
- **Feature Implementation**: Use Copilot to rapidly implement features from specifications with clean, tested, and documented code.
- **Debugging & Refactoring**: Leverage Copilot to detect root causes of bugs and apply targeted, minimal refactors.
- **Test Generation**: Utilize Copilot to automatically generate unit, integration, and end-to-end tests including edge cases.

### Strategic AI Lead (GPT-5.4)
- **Strategic Planning**: Use Copilot to generate project roadmaps, OKRs, and delivery plans aligned with stakeholder goals.
- **Cross-Agent Orchestration**: Leverage Copilot to design and document multi-agent workflows and task dependency graphs.
- **Quality Assurance**: Utilize Copilot to audit outputs from all agents for coherence, completeness, and alignment with project objectives.

## Best Practices
- **Iterative Feedback**: Regularly review and refine the suggestions provided by Copilot to ensure they align with project goals.
- **Contextual Awareness**: Provide context in comments or documentation to help Copilot generate more relevant suggestions.
- **Experimentation**: Don’t hesitate to experiment with different prompts to explore the full potential of Copilot’s capabilities.

## Conclusion
By following these guidelines, each role can effectively harness the power of GitHub Copilot to enhance their contributions to the Multi-Agent Development Environment project.
